description_long: This is a small, vintage-style biplane with a wooden fuselage and two parallel, straight wings stacked vertically. The wings and tail surfaces are painted a deep red, while the fuselage retains a natural wood finish, giving it a classic, handcrafted appearance. The airplane features green struts connecting the upper and lower wings, and a green vertical stabilizer and rudder at the tail. The nose is equipped with a small, green propeller and a pair of simple, fixed landing wheels. The tailplane is straight with a red horizontal stabilizer and green vertical fin, and the cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing. This aircraft is reminiscent of early 20th-century biplanes, often seen in airshows or as decorative models in urban parks or playgrounds.
description: A small, vintage-style red and wood biplane with green struts, a front propeller, and an open cockpit behind the upper wing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stacked red wings, green struts, and the wooden fuselage with the tail and horizontal stabilizer visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the wooden fuselage, the red wings, green tail fin, and the small front propeller.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full wingspan, green tail fin, and the open cockpit area behind the upper wing.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same features as view 1, with the red wings, wooden fuselage, and green tail surfaces.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by interplane struts; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a small tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, mid-fuselage.
